originalTitle: Kidnapping of foreigners soars in Africa's lawless Sahel region
title: Kidnapping of foreigners soars in Africa's lawless Sahel region
content: Growing insecurity in the Sahel made 2025 one of the worst years on record for the abduction of foreigners in Africa.

originalTitle: At least 55 Ghanaians killed in Russia-Ukraine war, minister says
title: Sad News from Far Away: Ghanaians Lost in a War
content:
What is this Sad News About?

Hello, my wonderful friends! Today, I have some news that might make us feel a little bit sad. It's about some people from a special country in Africa called Ghana. You see, there is a big fight happening very far away. We call this fight a "war." It's happening between two countries named Ukraine and Russia. It's a very serious problem when countries fight like this. A grown-up leader from Ghana has told us something important. He said that some people from Ghana have sadly lost their lives in this war. This news makes us all think about peace. We always hope for peace in the world, where everyone can be safe and happy. Losing people is always very sad for their families and friends. We care about all the people from Ghana.

Where is Ukraine and Russia?

Now, you might be wondering, "Where are these places, Ukraine and Russia?" Well, Ghana is in a beautiful part of Africa, right? Ukraine and Russia are very, very far away from Ghana. Imagine flying in an airplane for many, many hours! They are in a part of the world called Europe. They are not close to Ghana at all. But even though they are far, far away, what happens there can still touch people from Ghana and other places. The world is like one big neighborhood, and sometimes problems in one part can affect people everywhere. It's important to remember that we are all connected. **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, and Ghana is one of them.

Why Were Ghanaians There?

You might ask, "Why were people from Ghana in a place so far away where there's a war?" That's a very good question. Many young people from Ghana, just like grown-ups from other countries, sometimes travel to different places to study. They go to big schools called universities. Or sometimes, they go to work and help their families back home. So, some Ghanaians were living in Ukraine or Russia when the war started. They were trying to learn new things or build a better life. It was a big surprise when the war began. They never thought such a big fight would start. It made things very difficult and scary for them.

What Does "55 People" Mean?

The grown-up leader from Ghana said that 55 people from Ghana have sadly passed away. What does the number 55 mean? Imagine your classroom. Maybe there are 20 or 30 children in your class. Now, imagine *two whole classrooms* full of children, and then some more! That's how many people, 55, have sadly lost their lives. That is a very big number of people. It means many families are feeling very, very sad right now. Each person was special, with a family, friends, and dreams. This is the biggest number of people from any one African country to have lost their lives in this war. It's a very difficult thing for Ghana and all of Africa to hear. **Did you know?** Ghana is famous for its delicious cocoa! Cocoa beans are used to make chocolate. Yum!

We Feel Sad and Care

When we hear news like this, it's okay to feel sad. It's okay to feel a little bit worried. When people get hurt or die in a war, it is always a tragedy. It reminds us how important it is for everyone to live in peace. We want all our friends in Ghana and everywhere else to be safe. We send our love and prayers to the families of these 55 Ghanaians. We think about their moms, dads, brothers, sisters, and friends. They must be missing them very much. It's a time for us to remember to be kind to each other. We should always try to solve problems with talking, not fighting. That's what peace means.

What Can We Do?

So, what can we do when we hear such sad news from far away? Even though we are small, we can do big things! We can learn about peace. Peace means being friendly, sharing, and solving problems without anger. We can be kind to our friends at school. We can help our families at home. Every small act of kindness helps make the world a better place. We can also hope and pray for peace. We can wish for the fighting in Ukraine and Russia to stop soon. We hope that all the people who are still there can come home safely. We want everyone to live in a world where there are no wars, just happiness and friendship. **Did you know?** The flag of Ghana has three colours: red, gold, and green! Red is for the blood of those who died for independence, gold is for wealth, and green is for the forests and farms. There's also a black star for African freedom!

Let's Hope for Peace

It is very important for us to remember that even though sad things happen, we can always hope for better days. We hope that the leaders of Ukraine and Russia can find a way to stop fighting and make peace. We hope that no more people from Ghana or any other country will get hurt. We want everyone to be able to go to school, play with friends, and live happily without fear. Let's all think about peace today. Let's send happy thoughts and love to Ghana and all the people affected by the war. Together, we can hope for a peaceful world for everyone. Let's keep learning, keep growing, and always choose kindness.
summary: A leader from Ghana shared sad news that 55 people from Ghana have died in a far-away war between Ukraine and Russia, which makes us think about peace.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows how wars far away can still hurt people from Africa, reminding us that we all need to wish for peace and safety for everyone in the world.
